header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary:
I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Organizational Development professional who thrives on developing programs grounded in evidence, driving improvement via data analysis, and partnering across teams to enable people. 
Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people development—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Consult with {cross|executive leadership} to {prog|redesign accountability structures} and clarify {cross|cross-functional roles} in support of a 2-year strategic plan
bullets.reason: NEW bullet — surfaces exec consulting + cross-functional role clarity, directly maps Mercury's CS/Quality/Strategy partnership pillar

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ leave cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via {data|evidence-based insights}
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to {prog|policy redesigns and first aid training}
Conduct needs assessments with 25+ {cross|stakeholders} to {prog|identify learning gaps} and design 3 {prog|enablement programs} for product launch
bullets.original: Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders to identify learning gaps and design 3 new educational programs
bullets.reason: Swapped 'educational programs' → 'enablement programs for product launch' to mirror Mercury's CS enablement framing

bullets.text: Develop {coord|timelines, processes, and knowledge base} for 70+ {cross|cross-functional} staff, reducing onboarding by 75%
bullets.original: Develop guides and workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff, improving program knowledge and reducing onboarding time by 75%
bullets.reason: Honest reframe — 'timelines, processes, knowledge base' reflects what Jamie actually built (docs, protocols, resources). Maps JD's KM practices pillar without stretching to 'scalable systems'

bullets.text: Facilitate {prog|training webinars} for 600+ participants, leveraging {data|attendance and feedback} to refine delivery and drive 78% customer conversion
bullets.original: Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, enhancing program understanding and driving a 78% client conversion rate
bullets.reason: Honest reframe — 'leveraging attendance and feedback to refine delivery' reflects what Jamie actually did (tracked webinar data informally) instead of overclaiming 'completion metrics'. 78% conversion rate IS the metric

bullets.text: Manage 20+ {prog|programs} and 10+ vendors end-to-end, refining {coord|delivery strategy} and {data|auditing content} via {data|feedback analysis}
bullets.original: Manage 20+ programs and 10+ vendors, refining program positioning, delivery strategy, and operations via feedback analysis
bullets.reason: Per Jamie's edit — 'auditing content' as an action verb (parallel with refining) reads more direct and maps JD's training-material audit requirement

bullets.text: Analyzed 2,000+ {data|employee survey responses}, surfacing key engagement drivers to inform C-suite actions
bullets.original: Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers
bullets.reason: Reordered to lead — large-scale survey analysis is the strongest data/metrics signal for Mercury's learning-metrics pillar

bullets.text: Led a manager effectiveness initiative, translating {data|data} into an {prog|executive-ready presentation} that informed training strategy
bullets.original: Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming feedback data into actionable reports that informed engagement strategies
bullets.reason: Reframed to surface 'training strategy' (vs 'engagement strategies') — directly maps Mercury's L&D / training-effectiveness focus

bullets.text: Managed weekly {tools|SharePoint dashboards} tracking {data|people metrics, NPS, and survey insights} to inform recognition and engagement initiatives
bullets.original: Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
bullets.reason: NEW bullet — surfaces concrete KM tool (SharePoint), CS-relevant metric (NPS), and weekly cadence. Directly maps Mercury's KM practices and metrics pillars

bullets.text: Improved {ex|employee experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects in survey development, performance review, and {prog|L&D}
bullets.original: Improved employee experience via 5 cross-functional projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
bullets.reason: Tightened; reordered list to lead with survey/performance/L&D for direct relevance to Mercury's learning-metrics + program ownership

bullets.text: Partnered with HRBP team to {prog|design and pilot} an {prog|Inclusive Leadership workshop} for 12 senior leaders, later scaled to 23 global locations
bullets.original: Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
bullets.reason: Reframed to 'design and pilot' + 'scaled' — directly maps to Mercury's scalable learning systems requirement

bullets.text: Optimized {coord|onboarding processes} across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
bullets.reason: Moved up — onboarding/process work parallels Mercury's KM and scalable enablement systems

bullets.text: Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
bullets.reason: Moved down — strong stakeholder coordination signal but less directly tied to learning programs

bullets.text: Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Engaged global talent via personalized {cross|communication and customer service}, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
bullets.reason: Moved to lead — customer service/communication signal directly supports Mercury's CS-context bridge

bullets.text: Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
bullets.reason: Moved to last — broadest/least specific bullet

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Educational Psychology, Practicum in Education Studies, Learning and Motivation, Psychological Testing

projects: Consulting Projects: Enhanced Elanco Animal Health employer branding & talent attraction strategy via survey design/execution
Master's Capstone: Examined non-monetary factors shaping employer attractiveness for YouTube via mixed-method research
Scholarships: Freeman Asian Scholarship (4-year full-tuition), Tuck Bridge Scholarship (full)
skills: Technical Skills: LMS (Canvas, Moodle) · Project Management (Asana, Airtable) · Knowledge Management (Notion, SharePoint, Google Workspace) · Data Analysis (Excel, Google Sheets, SPSS) · Design Tools (Canva, Adobe InDesign) · GenAI Tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude)
Certifications: HRCI Human Resource Associate Certificate (in progress)

jd.meta: Mercury — Customer Support Learning & Enablement Specialist · SF / NYC / Portland OR / Remote (US/Canada) · $95,600–$132,800 (US) · CS Operations

sections.title: Role Overview
sections.content: Designs and scales {prog|learning initiatives} across Mercury's Customer Support function. Owns {coord|end-to-end enablement programs}, defines timelines, drives coordination, establishes {data|learning metrics}, creates {prog|engaging instructional content}, develops {coord|scalable learning systems}, improves {coord|knowledge management practices}, and audits training materials. Partners with {cross|CS, Quality, and Strategy} to identify performance gaps and deliver targeted learning solutions.

items.text: Manage {coord|end-to-end enablement programs} — define timelines and drive coordination
Establish {data|learning metrics} to measure training effectiveness
Create engaging {prog|instructional content} across multiple formats
Partner with {cross|CS, Quality, Strategy} to identify performance gaps
3+ years in L&D, instructional design, or enablement (ideally CS / regulated environments) ✅
Demonstrated end-to-end program ownership with stakeholder + timeline management ✅
Data-informed approach with experience defining success metrics ✅
Strong instructional design and facilitation capabilities ⚠️ (facilitation strong; ID limited)
Familiarity with LMS platforms (WorkRamp, Articulate) ⚠️ (Canvas/Moodle yes; Articulate no)

sections.title: ✅ H1B Status
sections.content: Mercury Technologies Inc has confirmed LCA filings on h1bdata.info (mostly SF Senior Software Engineer roles). Sponsorship infrastructure exists; non-tech sponsorship not directly verified. Strong sponsor track record.

changeLog.change: Reframed L&D identity: 'building scalable learning programs', 'establishing metrics that demonstrate impact', 'partnering across teams to enable people' — directly maps Mercury's three pillars (program ownership, metrics, cross-functional).
Kept Pair A 'Upskill/Empower' bullets — directly demonstrates internal-team training and data-driven training outcomes per L&D playbook.

Bullet 1: 'enablement programs for product launch' — surfaces JD's enablement framing. Bullet 2: 'scalable knowledge systems' + 'onboarding documentation' — KM + scalable systems. Bullet 3: 'engagement and completion metrics' — explicit learning metrics. Bullet 4: 'end-to-end' + 'content audits' — program ownership + training audits.
Lead with internal training consultations (most L&D-adjacent), then L&D-focused cross-functional projects, then data/manager work.
Lead reframed to 'design and pilot... scaled to 23 locations' — directly maps to scalable learning systems. Onboarding moved up (KM parallel).
Lead with customer-service/communication bullet — bridge to Mercury's customer support context. Then events facilitation, JD drafting, broad HR last.

Led with LMS (Canvas, Moodle) per JD. Added Knowledge Management (Notion, SharePoint, Google Workspace) for JD's KM emphasis. Kept Design Tools (Canva, Adobe InDesign) honest — did NOT claim Articulate / WorkRamp since Jamie has no hands-on experience.

Two acknowledged gaps: (1) Articulate / WorkRamp specifically — listed Canvas/Moodle truthfully. (2) Pure instructional design depth — Jamie's strength is program ownership + facilitation, not authoring. Address in cover letter / interview, do not overclaim in resume.
